body.title: Replace get_attractors() full-table-scan with hot_attractors/TWM read
body.result: d8d80b2b — module-level _ATTRACTOR_CACHE with 60s TTL. Follow-on for proper hot_attractors subsystem flagged in docstring. 5 tests pass.
body.description:
cortex.get_attractors() (cortex.py:5249) does a full-table scan: SELECT m.id FROM memories LEFT JOIN interpretive_edges WHERE memory_type NOT IN ('PROCEDURAL') ORDER BY activation_count * (1 + COUNT(ie.id)). Fires 891 times (avg 181ms, worst 807ms). A comment on cortex.py:1635 already names the fix: 'Real fix: read from hot_attractors/TWM'. Replace the full scan with a TWM read — cortex.twm_get_attractor() or the hot_attractors index — so attractor lookup is O(1) not O(n). Pre-approved by Akien 2026-04-22 for touching cortex.py (HIGH-inertia) — reason: performance blocker for Igor helpfulness goal.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (get_attractors at line 5249, internal call at 5321). Update cortex.py top-of-file docstring to document the TWM-backed attractor path and remove the 'Real fix' TODO comment. graph_ops.py:32 calls get_attractors as a tool — no change needed there, just calls the new impl.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite: reads TWM (in-process) and hot_attractors (Postgres via existing table) — no SQLite, no new tables. oop-first: replacing one read strategy inside existing method, no new shared state. docs-in-code: update cortex.py:5249 docstring. test-plan-or-why-not: applies.
**Scope boundary:** IN — replace the full-scan inside get_attractors(). OUT — restructuring hot_attractors/TWM, changing what 'attractor' means semantically, callers (graph_ops.py tool stays unchanged).
**Test plan:** Integration test on real Postgres — after fix, db_queries.log shows 0 new entries matching the full-scan pattern per session. Unit test — mock hot_attractors/TWM with 5 known IDs, assert get_attractors() returns them without hitting memories table.
